mapping.a_element: error-graph of codons (codons joined when likely to be confused)
smooth low-lying modes of the Laplacian of errors that govern the emergent code
mapping.b_element: eigenspace of the second-smallest eigenvalue λ2 of the graph Laplacian

mapping.a_element: the emergent genetic code as a map of codons to amino-acids built from those Laplacian modes
mapping.b_element: the tight mapping of G into m-dimensional Euclidean space defined by the λ2-eigenspace

mapping.a_element: number of amino-acids = target dimension of the code map
mapping.b_element: multiplicity m / substantial dimension of the tight mapping

mapping.a_element: upper bound on the number of amino-acids from the classical map-coloring problem
mapping.b_element: Colin de Verdière's upper bound on λ2-multiplicity via chromatic number chr and genus γ(G)

brief_justification: Both run on spectral graph theory: the low eigenmodes of a graph Laplacian embed the graph's vertices into a low-dimensional Euclidean space, and that space's dimension is capped by a coloring/chromatic invariant. Paper A uses the smooth Laplacian modes of the codon error-graph to build the genetic code and bounds the amino-acid count by the map-coloring problem, exactly paralleling Paper B's λ2-eigenspace tight mapping bounded by the chromatic-number/genus Colin de Verdière bound.
